### Evidence 2187: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E19
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Now just a couple of other things to note about Helm.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2188: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E20
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So Helm isn't just about customizing your configurations on a per environment basis, Helm is actually a complete package manager for your application.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 2189: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E21
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So think of like either YUM or APT for a Linux system, it kind of operates the same way for your Kubernetes application.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2190: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And Helm is a little bit more complex, but it also comes with a few extra features that Kustomize doesn't have, so things like conditionals, loops, functions, and hooks, so you do get a little bit more functionality with Helm.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2191: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, with that extra functionality does come some extra complexity.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2192: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E24
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So, you know, when you're dealing with these Helm templates, technically they're not valid YAML because they are using that Go templating syntax, and so if you take a look at some of the Helm charts out there, you'll see that they are actually really, really difficult to read, just due to that temping syntax where everything is a variable and it just becomes hard to figure out like, what exactly is our chart doing?
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2193: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E25
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: What values can I replace?
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2194: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E26
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Overall, it just becomes a little bit of a challenge to read those charts, and that's one of the perks about Kustomize, is that Kustomize is very simple.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2195: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E27
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: It's very easy to read, it's just regular YAML.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2196: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E28
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The regular base configurations is just regular Kubernetes configs, and then the overlays, where we go to modify it on a per environment basis, that's all valid YAML as well.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2197: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/264_Kustomize vs Helm.extraction.md::E29
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 264_Kustomize vs Helm.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So I think one of the big trade offs is, you know, Kustomize is easier and simpler, whereas Helm is a little bit more complex, but it has more features as well, so there are trade offs and you definitely wanna think about what each one brings to the table when you decide on what is the tool that you're going to use for your specific project.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2260: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/267_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E12

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so what this pipe utility does is it takes the output of the first command, which is the kustomize build, k8s or whatever to the is to the left of the pipe utility.

### Evidence 2261: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/267_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 267_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And it redirects the output of first command into the input of the second command, which is the command to the right of the pipe utility.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2262: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/267_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E14

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we're basically running the traditional kubectl apply -f, but we're applying a file that comes from the output of the first command, which is the kustomize build k8s command.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2263: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/267_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Implementation Step

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So that's all it's doing is taking the output of one command and applying it as the input of the other command.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 269_Managing Directories.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And this is fine, however, as we grow out the number of directories or sub directories that we have, it's gonna start to become a little bit of a pain having to go into each subdirectory and doing a kubectl apply because then every time we make changes, every time we want to, apply our configs or delete our configs, we'd have to go inside each one of our sub directories and make sure we run that command inside each subdirectory.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2636: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E1
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: (gentle music) Instructor: In this section, we're gonna take a look at patches.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2637: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so kustomize patches provide another method to modifying Kubernetes configs.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2638: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E3
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, unlike common transformers, patches provide a more surgical approach to targeting one or more specific sections in a Kubernetes resource.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2639: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E4
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So basically, if you want to apply a configuration across the board, like adding a label or assigning a namespace to all of your Kubernetes objects, you're gonna use a common transformer.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2640: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, if you wanna apply or change something on one specific object or just a couple of objects, you would use a kustomize patch.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2641: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/276_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics / 276_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So things like updating the number of replicas in a deployment, you would use a kustomize patch to match a specific object and then change that value.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 2930: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/281_Overlays.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Implementation Step

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: As mentioned before in one of the first lectures of this course, Kustomize was created to allow us to take a base Kubernetes config and customize it on a per-environment basis.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2931: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/281_Overlays.extraction.md::E3

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so if we have a development environment and a staging environment and a production environment, we may wanna tweak certain properties on a per-environment basis, and that's where Kustomize really does come into play.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 2932: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/13_(2025 Updates)Kustomize Basics/281_Overlays.extraction.md::E4

### Evidence 3117: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests/15_Other Topics/299_JSON Path in Kubernetes.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 15_Other Topics / 299_JSON Path in Kubernetes.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When you're working with production environments for Kubernetes, you will need to view information about hundreds of nodes and thousands of objects like deployments, pods and replica sets and services and secrets, etc. and you will be using kube control utility to view information about these objects.

- Type: Architecture
- Source: certified-kubernetes-administrator-with-practice-tests / 15_Other Topics / 299_JSON Path in Kubernetes.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A Jsonpath query of items of star dot metadata dot name with the cube control get nodes command gives us the names of the nodes in the cluster, a query of items dot status dot node info dot architecture returns me the hardware architecture of the nodes notes and status dot capacity dot CPU returns me the count of CPUs on these nodes.

### Evidence 3286: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/02_Core Concepts/007_Recap - Kubernetes Architecture.extraction.md::E57
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 02_Core Concepts / 007_Recap - Kubernetes Architecture.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And finally, we also need to learn a little bit about one of the command line utilities known as the Kube command line tool or kubectl or kube control, as it is also called.

- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 02_Core Concepts / 012_Recap - Pods with YAML.txt

- Content: The replica set selector also provides many other options for matching labels that were not available in a replication controller, and as always, to create a replica set, run the kubectl create command, providing the definition file as input, and to see the created replicas run the kubectl get replica set command.

### Evidence 3582: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/02_Core Concepts/024_Recap - Deployments.extraction.md::E11

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 02_Core Concepts / 024_Recap - Deployments.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Finally, say, for example, you would like to make multiple changes to your environment, such as upgrading the underlying web server versions, as well as scaling your environment and also modifying the resource allocations, etc. you do not want to apply each change immediately after the command is run.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Configuration, CKA-CKAD

### Evidence 3583: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/02_Core Concepts/024_Recap - Deployments.extraction.md::E12

- Content: If I were to set it up manually, I would start with an operating system like Ubuntu, then update the source repositories using the APT command, then install dependencies using the APT command, then install Python dependencies using the pip command, then copy over the source code of my application to a location like OPT, and then finally run the web server using the flask command.

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Unlike virtual machines, containers are not meant to host an operating system.

### Evidence 3815: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/03_Configuration/043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 03_Configuration / 043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.txt

- Content: Containers are meant to run a specific task or process, such as to host an instance of a web server or application server or a database, or simply to carry out some kind of computation or analysis.

### Evidence 3816: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/03_Configuration/043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.extraction.md::E14

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 3817: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/03_Configuration/043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 03_Configuration / 043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: A container only lives as long as the process inside it is alive.

### Evidence 3818: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/03_Configuration/043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 03_Configuration / 043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.txt

### Evidence 3819: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/03_Configuration/043_Commands and Arguments in Docker.extraction.md::E17

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: When we ran the ubuntu container earlier, Docker created a container from the ubuntu image and launched the bash program.

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And what we want to do is we want to have all of the Kubernetes configs for each environment within their respective folder.

### Evidence 4803: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/174_Kustomize Problem Statement & idealogy.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Implementation Step

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we're essentially duplicating the configs across all of the three different environments and then just modifying the attributes on a per-environment basis so that we can have different replicas for each environment.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4804: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/174_Kustomize Problem Statement & idealogy.extraction.md::E17

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so what this is gonna look like is, in the deployment file in the dev folder, we're going to make sure that the replicas is set to 1, and in the staging folder the replicas will be set to 2, and in the production the replicas is gonna be set to 5.

### Evidence 4956: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/177_kustomization.yaml file.extraction.md::E28
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 177_kustomization.yaml file.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We learned that kustomize will look for a kustomization file, which is going to contain the two following things; a list of all of the Kubernetes resources or manifests that kustomize should manage, as well as all of the customizations that should be applied.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4957: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/177_kustomization.yaml file.extraction.md::E29
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 177_kustomization.yaml file.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Then, when we run the kustomize build command, this is going to take all of the resources, all of the manifests that were defined in the kustomization.yaml file, and it's going to apply all of the transformations.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4958: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/177_kustomization.yaml file.extraction.md::E30

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But it's important to understand that when we run this command, it does not actually apply or deploy the Kubernetes resources.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4959: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/177_kustomization.yaml file.extraction.md::E31
- Type: Concept

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 4960: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/177_kustomization.yaml file.extraction.md::E32

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 177_kustomization.yaml file.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: If we want to actually apply these configs to a cluster, we actually have to take the output of this command and redirect it to the kubectl apply command.

### Evidence 4965: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E4

### Evidence 4966: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E5

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So you might be thinking, "Well, how exactly do we apply these configs?
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4967: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We're making use of this awesome utility called Kustomize, but we can't actually apply it to our Kubernetes Cluster." Well, the command that you actually have to run to run Kustomize, build all the configs, and then apply those configs, is this following command right here.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration, Architecture

### Evidence 4968: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: It's pretty long, but let me explain what it does.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4969: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So when you run this command, it's going to make use of the Linux Pipe Utility.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4970: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And this isn't something that's specific to Kubernetes or Kustomize, this is a feature of Linux, or really any bash or shell.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4971: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so what this Pipe Utility does is it takes the output of the first command, which is the Kustomize build k8s or whatever is to the left of the Pipe Utility, and it redirects the output of that first command into the input of the second command, which is the command to the right of the Pipe Utility.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4972: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we're basically running the traditional kubectl apply -f, but we're applying a file that comes from the output of the first command, which is the kustomize build k8s command.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4973: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E12
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So that's all it's doing.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4974: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: It's taking the output of one command and applying it as the input of the other command.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4975: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E14
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so that allows us to make use of the usual kubectl apply, and that creates our nginx deployment, as well as the nginx service.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Services, Configuration

### Evidence 4976: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And if you wanna take a look at how to do this natively with just the kubectl tool, you can do kubectl apply, and then you do a -k instead of a -f, and then you provide the directory where the Kustomization.yaml file exists.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4977: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So that's how we apply these resources.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 4978: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E17

### Evidence 4979: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E18
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 178_Kustomize Output.txt

### Evidence 4980: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/178_Kustomize Output.extraction.md::E19

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 5173: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/184_Image Transformers.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 184_Image Transformers.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The image transformer is what's going to allow us to modify an image that a specific deployment or container is going to use through Kustomize.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 5333: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E1
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: (upbeat music) Announcer: In this section, we're gonna take a look at patches.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5334: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so, customized patches provide another method to modifying Kubernetes configs.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5335: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E3
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, unlike common transformers, patches provide a more surgical approach to targeting one or more specific sections in a Kubernetes resource.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5336: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E4
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So basically, if you want to apply a configuration across the board, like adding a label or assigning a namespace to all of your Kubernetes objects, you're gonna use a common transformer.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5337: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: However, if you wanna apply or change something on one specific object or just a couple of objects, you would use a customized patch.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5338: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So things like updating the number of replicas in a deployment, you would use a customized patch to match a specific object and then change that value.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 5339: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Now to create a patch, we have to provide three different parameters.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5340: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The first one is operation type.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5341: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So what exactly do we want to do?
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5342: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so, I've listed out the three most common operations.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5343: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Keep in mind there are a couple more, but they're not as frequently used.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5344: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E12
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we have add, remove, and replace.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5345: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E13
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So add, you'd perform an operation, an add operation.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5346: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E14
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: If we have a list of containers in a deployment, and let's say you wanted to add another container to that list, that would be an add operation.
- Multi-concept tags: Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 5347: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Remove would be the opposite.

### Evidence 5348: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E16
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Let's say you wanted to remove a container from the list or even remove a label.

- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: It could be removing anything.

- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And then finally, we have replace.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5351: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E19
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So replace is taking a value that's provided and then swapping it out with another value.

### Evidence 5352: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E20
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So if the base configs have a replica count of five and you say, oh, I actually want 10, you could do a replace and replace the replica to a value of 10.

### Evidence 5353: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E21

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Then after we specify the operation, we have to provide a target.

### Evidence 5354: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt

### Evidence 5355: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E23
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt

- Content: And so we can match on a couple of different properties like kind or the version or the name of the object or the namespace label selector or annotation selector.

### Evidence 5356: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E24

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 187_Patches Intro.txt

### Evidence 5359: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/187_Patches Intro.extraction.md::E27

### Evidence 5695: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E2
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so a component provides the ability to define a reusable piece of configuration logic that can be included in multiple overlays.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5696: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E3
- Type: Exam Tip
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so, you know, these are gonna be really useful in situations where you have an application that supports multiple optional features that need to be enabled only in a subset of the overlays.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5697: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E4
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And that's important to understand, a subset of the overlays.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5698: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E5
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Let's say we have a certain set of configurations that we need to enable a certain feature.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5699: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E6
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: If we wanted it enabled across all of the overlays, we would just put it in our base configs.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5700: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E7
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: But let's say we didn't want it enabled in all of our overlays, only in a subset of them.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5701: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E8
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Well, we could do that pretty easily just by defining them in each one of the overlays, but then now we're copying and pasting all of the configs.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5702: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E9
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so that's not a scalable solution.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5703: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E10
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We usually try to avoid copying and pasting things like that 'cause a lot of times, it leads to some config drift, if you make changes in one, you forget to make changes in the other.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5704: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E11
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So components are just reusable blocks of Kubernetes configs, that's all it is.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5705: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E12
- Type: Warning/Pitfall
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Don't complicate it, it's just all of the resources for a specific feature, all of the patches, all of the config maps, secrets, and any other Kubernetes-related configs associated with the feature, they go into a component, and then you can enable it in multiple overlays, that's it.
- Multi-concept tags: Security-RBAC, Configuration

### Evidence 5706: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E13

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So let's take a look at a visual example because I think once you visually see how all of this kind of fits together, you'll have a much better understanding of how components work.

### Evidence 5707: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E14

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Now, let's say our application can be deployed in three different variations.

### Evidence 5708: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E15
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: We've got the development variation, the premium one for premium customers, and then a self-hosted version for any one of our customers that wants to self-host our application.

### Evidence 5709: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E16

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we've got those three folders, and those would represent the three different overlays in a customized application.

### Evidence 5710: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E17
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And then we obviously have our folder for the base configuration that's gonna be shared across all three overlays.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5711: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E18

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt

- Content: Now, let's say our application has a couple of optional features and one of those is caching.

### Evidence 5712: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E19

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And let's say that only the premium and the self-hosted version of our application should have caching enabled.

### Evidence 5713: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E20

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 5714: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E21

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So only these two versions are gonna have caching enabled.

### Evidence 5715: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E22
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 5716: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E23

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 5717: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E24

### Evidence 5719: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E26

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt

- Content: And the problem with doing that is all three overlays will get the configuration, but remember, only self-hosted and premium should get the caching configuration.

### Evidence 5721: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E28

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript

### Evidence 5723: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E30

### Evidence 5724: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E31

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt

### Evidence 5725: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E32

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt

### Evidence 5726: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E33
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And let's say we, over time, decide to add a few more variations to how we can deploy our application, we would have to remember to copy it into all of those.

### Evidence 5727: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E34
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So like most other things, like developing any applications, you wanna avoid copying and pasting whenever you can.

### Evidence 5728: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E35
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And so that's why components were created, they were kind of created so that we could have a reusable block of code that we can apply in several different overlays.

### Evidence 5729: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E36
- Type: Best Practice
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we could take our caching configuration, all the Kubernetes config for that, and create a component, a customized component, and then we can just import that component into all of the different overlays that should be using it.

### Evidence 5730: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E37

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So let's take a look at how that would work.

### Evidence 5731: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E38

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So we've got the exact same folder structure with our three overlays and our base configuration, but now we're gonna create an extra folder and we're gonna store all of our components here.

### Evidence 5732: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E39
- Type: Concept
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And there's going to be two different components, caching and database, so inside the caching folder, we're gonna have all of the Kubernetes configs that are associated with the caching feature.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5733: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E40
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So like I said, that's going to be probably like a Redis instance with all of the configurations and secrets for the authentication side of our Redis database, as well as anything else that we need for our Redis database, as well as we're gonna have a folder for our external database feature, and that's going to contain all of the configs that we need to implement that specific feature.
- Multi-concept tags: Security-RBAC, Configuration

### Evidence 5734: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E41

- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So they're all isolated into their own folder.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5735: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E42
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Then what we can do is all of the overlays that need the caching functionality, they can just import that component, so they just have one line in their configs that say, hey, I want to import caching.
- Multi-concept tags: Configuration

### Evidence 5736: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E43
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: certified-kubernetes-application-developer / 11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics / 194_Components.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And then for all of the overlays that need the database, they can just import the database component.

### Evidence 5737: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E44

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: So all of the logic for the caching functionality sits in one spot in that caching folder, and all of the logic for the external database functionality sits in the database folder inside the components directory.

### Evidence 5738: certified-kubernetes-application-developer/11_(2025 Updates) Kustomize Basics/194_Components.extraction.md::E45

- Content: Our developers could now get started with a simple docker run command, irrespective of what the underlying operating system they run.

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Setting up these container environments is hard as they are very low level, and that is where Docker offers a high level tool with several powerful functionalities, making it really easy for end users like us to understand how Docker works, let us revisit some basic concepts of operating systems first.

### Evidence 6556: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E62
- Type: Implementation Step

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: If you would like to see the pods that were created by the replication controller, run the kubectl get pods command and you will see three pods running.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6557: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E89
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: And as always, to create a replica set, run the kubectl create command, providing the definition file as input and to see the created replicas run the kubectl get replica set command to get list of pods.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6558: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E90
- Type: Concept
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: Simply run the kube control get pods command.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6559: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E124
- Type: Implementation Step
- Source: learn-kubernetes / 05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments / 026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.txt
- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The first is to update the number of replicas in the definition file to six, then run the kubectl apply command to specify the same file using the f parameter, and that will update the replica set to have six replicas.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6560: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E125

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: The second way to do it is to run the kube control scale command.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration

### Evidence 6561: learn-kubernetes/05_Kubernetes Concepts - Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments/026_Replication Controllers and ReplicaSets.extraction.md::E129

- Line Context: Lines 1-1 from transcript
- Content: In other words, the number of replicas in the replica set definition file will still be three, even though you scaled your replica set to have six replicas using the Kube control scale command and the file as input.
- Multi-concept tags: Pods, Deployments, Configuration
